feature.name: QuickBooks Migration Requirements
feature.description: Document QuickBooks integration migration from FileMaker to backend/Supabase

standing_constraints: DO NOT modify backend infrastructure or database schema
Follow Backend Change Protocol for any backend requirements
Document only - do not implement code changes yet
Reference existing code files with line numbers

tasks.title: Create README.md with QuickBooks overview
tasks.description: Create requirements/quickbooks/README.md documenting current QuickBooks integration, FileMaker dependencies, and migration objectives

tasks.title: Document current QuickBooks implementation
tasks.description: Create current-implementation.md analyzing initializeQuickBooks function, FileMaker script behavior, existing quickbooksApi.js client, and UI integration points

tasks.title: Map QuickBooks data model requirements
tasks.description: Create data-model-mapping.md documenting token storage requirements, organization configuration, QB entity mappings, and Supabase schema needs

tasks.title: Define QuickBooks API contracts
tasks.description: Create api-contracts.md specifying backend endpoints for QB initialization, OAuth flow, token refresh, invoice generation, customer sync, and testing operations

tasks.title: Document authorization requirements
tasks.description: Create authorization.md covering OAuth flow, token lifecycle, organization scoping, RLS policies, and security considerations

tasks.title: Create migration plan
tasks.description: Create migration-plan.md with step-by-step approach to migrate from FileMaker scripts to backend API, including rollback procedures and cutover strategy

tasks.title: Define acceptance criteria and test plan
tasks.description: Create acceptance-criteria.md with test cases covering OAuth flow, token refresh, invoice generation, error handling, retry logic, and edge cases

tasks.title: Create backend change request document
tasks.description: Create BACKEND_CHANGE_REQUEST_001_QUICKBOOKS_MIGRATION.md following the backend change protocol with SQL, API contracts, testing requirements, and rollback plan
